title: Implement Git Status Integration
description: Show git status in the file browser. Use libgit2 wrapper or shell out to git command. Display file status with color coding: modified (yellow), added (green), deleted (red), untracked (grey). Show current branch in toolbar.
agent_output: The core implementation (GitStatusManager, FileBrowserView git UI, FileNode git status property) was already fully implemented by a previous session. This ticket's work was completing the wiring: `GitStatusManager.swift` existed on disk but was missing from the Xcode project — it would never compile. The `gitStatusRefreshNeeded` notification was referenced but undefined, causing a compile error. Tests were added following the existing `RickTerminalTests/` pattern. Preview crash was fixed as a bonus.
review_notes: AGENT FAILURE: Agent timed out after 600s. Consider splitting the ticket.
files_touched: RickTerminal.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` — Added `GitStatusManager.swift` in 4 locations: PBXBuildFile entry, PBXFileReference entry, FileBrowser group membership, and Sources build phase
RickTerminal/Keyboard/KeyboardShortcutManager.swift` — Added missing `gitStatusRefreshNeeded` notification name that was referenced in `FileBrowserView.swift` but never defined

RickTerminalTests/GitStatusManagerTests.swift` — New test file with 18 test cases covering status colors, badges, repository detection, status-for-path, uncommitted count, auto-refresh timer, git command execution, and notification name
RickTerminal/FileBrowser/FileBrowserView.swift` — Fixed `FileBrowserView_Previews` to include `EditorManager` and `CTOEventBridge` environment objects (previously would crash Xcode previews)
